portfolio_decision.key_risk: Exceptional valuations and growth assumptions are highly vulnerable to demand normalization, competitive pressure, or macroeconomic contraction in AI/data center markets.
portfolio_decision.entry_conditions: (1) P/E ratio becomes calculable and falls below 35x forward earnings; (2) Data reliability improves to ≥75%; (3) Customer concentration risk by segment is disclosed, showing no single segment >60% of revenue; (4) Operating margin sustains above 50% through a full business cycle; (5) Analyst consensus achieves at least 4 of 5 "Enter" or "Conditional Interest" votes.
portfolio_decision.exit_triggers: (1) Revenue growth declines below 30% YoY; (2) Operating margin falls below 45%; (3) Data center customer concentration exceeds 70% of revenue; (4) Quarterly guidance miss >10%; (5) Major competitive loss to AMD or emerging AI chip vendors; (6) Macroeconomic recession signals materialize.
portfolio_decision.rationale: NVIDIA exhibits exceptional operational performance (85% revenue growth, 66% operating margin) but presents unacceptable analytical uncertainty for entry: P/E unavailability, 55% data reliability, and analyst consensus divided across lower conviction levels. The extraordinary growth and margins suggest significant valuation risk with limited margin of safety. A watchlist position allows capture of upside while waiting for either valuation compression, improved data transparency, or confirmed sustainable competitive moats before committing capital.
